title: Implementation: New templates
description: # Plan: New Template Library ## Background The template library currently covers 19 slide layouts. This plan adds 15 new templates in three focused PRs, grouped by semantic role. Each PR is self-contained: it creates the template files, wires the smoke tests, and updates CLAUDE.md. ### Research notes (patterns every implementer must follow) Before writing any code, read the templates listed below. They establish every convention used here. **Full-bleed slides** (no `SlideLayout`, manage own padding): - `TitleSlide` — left accent bar + `TitleChrome` + `64px 80px` padding, `maxWidth: 920px` - `SectionTitleSlide` — same shell + `fitFontSize` + split-flap animation - `TheEndSlide` — centered flex, radial vignette, per-character `letter-rise` animation **Content slides** (wrap with `SlideLayout`): - `BulletSlide` — minimal, shows canonical `SlideLayout + header` usage - `CardSlide` — staggered flip animation, hard-coded non-theme colors (justified by physical metaphor) - `CompareSlide` — shows animated row-by-row reveal **Animation conventions:** - One-shot stagger: `animationDelay = index * Nms`, applied via `style` - CSS animations are defined in `tailwind.config.ts` (`keyframes` + `animation` map), referenced as `animate-*` class names - New animations must be added to `tailwind.config.ts`; never use raw `@keyframes` in component files - For export safety: if an animation loops, gate the loop on `isExportMode` from `../../src/utils/export` **Color/token rules:** - Content slides: always `bg-background`, `text-slide-text`, `text-muted`, `text-accent`, `bg-accent`, `bg-surface`, `border-surface` - Hard-coded hex values are only acceptable when the visual metaphor demands it (playing card faces, photo placeholders) — document the exception with a comment - CSS variable form for non-Tailwind contexts (SVG fill, third-party props): `rgb(var(--color-accent))` etc. **Props conventions:** - Content slides: `header?: ReactNode` (rendered by `SlideLayout`) - Full-bleed slides: no `header` prop — direct string props only - Array items are typed with named interfaces exported from the same file - All props are typed; no `any` **Files per template:** 1. `src/templates/[name]/[Name]Slide.tsx` — main component + exported prop types 2. `src/templates/[name]/example.tsx` — fully filled-in reference slide (never rendered by default) **After each PR:** - Add a smoke-test case per new template to `src/templates/smoke.test.tsx` - Update the Template Catalog and Template Decision Guide table in `CLAUDE.md` - Run `npm run build` — fix all TypeScript errors before considering done - Run `npm run export -- --deck= --format=png` (or create a throwaway demo deck) to visually validate --- ## Task 1 — Structural / Framing Slides Five templates that orient the audience: a visual section break, agenda, table of contents, closing slide, and pull quote. ### 1a. `SectionDividerSlide` **When to use:** A full-bleed visual pause between major sections. Accepts a custom background color or image. Unlike `SectionTitleSlide` (which is text-focused with split-flap animation), this one is image/color-first — the title floats over the visual. **Shell:** Full-bleed (no `SlideLayout`). Pattern: `TitleSlide`/`SectionTitleSlide`. ```tsx export interface SectionDividerSlideProps { title: string eyebrow?: string subtitle?: string /** CSS color string — used as background when no backgroundImage is provided */ backgroundColor?: string /** URL to a background image (fills the slide, dimmed by overlay) */ backgroundImage?: string /** 0–1 overlay opacity to ensure text legibility over images. Default 0.55 */ overlayOpacity?: number } ``` **Visual design:** - Root: `w-full h-full relative overflow-hidden flex` - Background layer: `absolute inset-0` — either `bg-background` (default), solid `backgroundColor`, or `` / CSS `backgroundImage` with `object-fit: cover` - Semi-transparent dark overlay (`rgba(0,0,0,overlayOpacity)`) over the image so text is always legible - Left accent bar: `w-1 bg-accent flex-shrink-0` - `TitleChrome` for dot-grid texture - Content: `relative z-10 flex flex-col justify-center`, padding `64px 80px`, `maxWidth: 820px` - Eyebrow: 12px mono, `text-accent`, `letterSpacing: '0.28em'` - Title: large display font, `clamp(48px, 6vw, 80px)`, bold, `text-slide-text` - Subtitle: `text-muted`, 20px body, appears with `animate-subtitle-rise` - No animation on the title itself (static, unlike `SectionTitleSlide`) — keeps it composable with any background --- ### 1b. `AgendaSlide` **When to use:** Numbered list of topics at the start of a deck or section, with optional time estimates. **Shell:** `SlideLayout` with `header?: ReactNode`. ```tsx export interface AgendaItem { label: string /** Optional time estimate, e.g. "10 min" */ time?: string } export interface AgendaSlideProps { header?: ReactNode items: AgendaItem[] } ``` **Visual design:** - Identical row structure to `BulletSlide`: `flex items-center gap-6 py-5 border-b border-surface first:border-t` - Left column (fixed width ~48px): sequential number `01`, `02` … in `font-mono text-accent`, 13px, opacity 0.8 - Center column (`flex-1`): topic label in `font-body text-slide-text`, 21px - Right column (optional, `flex-shrink-0`): time in `font-mono text-muted`, 13px, `letterSpacing: '0.08em'` — only rendered when at least one item has a `time` - No animation needed — agenda is read, not revealed --- ### 1c. `TableOfContentsSlide` **When to use:** A richer section overview than `AgendaSlide` — visual grid with section numbers, titles, optional icons, and optional section subtitles. Works well as the second slide of a deck. **Shell:** `SlideLayout` with `header?: ReactNode`. ```tsx export interface TocItem { title: string icon?: ReactNode subtitle?: string } export interface TableOfContentsSlideProps { header?: ReactNode items: TocItem[] /** 2 or 3 columns. Default: 2 for ≤4 items, 3 for 5+ items */ columns?: 2 | 3 } ``` **Visual design:** - `flex-1 grid` with `grid-cols-2` or `grid-cols-3`, `gap-6` - Each cell: `bg-surface rounded-lg p-6 flex flex-col gap-3` - Section number: auto-generated `01`, `02` … displayed in `font-mono text-accent`, 13px, opacity 0.7 (top-left of cell) - Optional icon: `text-accent`, 24px, sits beside number - Title: `font-display font-bold text-slide-text`, 22px - Subtitle (optional): `font-body text-muted`, 14px - Cells fade up staggered: `animate-fade-up` with `animationDelay: i * 80ms` — the `fade-up` keyframe already exists in `tailwind.config.ts` --- ### 1d. `ClosingSlide` **When to use:** The final human-facing slide — "thank you", call to action, and/or contact info. Mirrors `TitleSlide` energy. Distinct from `TheEndSlide` (which is purely decorative cinema-style). **Shell:** Full-bleed (no `SlideLayout`). Pattern: `TitleSlide`. ```tsx export interface ContactInfo { email?: string website?: string linkedIn?: string } export interface ClosingSlideProps { /** Main headline. Default: "Thank You" */ headline?: string /** Secondary line below the headline */ subtitle?: string /** Optional call-to-action phrase */ cta?: string contact?: ContactInfo } ``` **Visual design:** - Same shell as `TitleSlide`: left accent bar + `TitleChrome` + content area `padding: 64px 80px`, `maxWidth: 920px`, vertically centered - Headline: `font-display font-bold text-slide-text`, `clamp(72px, 8vw, 108px)`, with `animate-letter-rise` stagger on each character (reuse `TheEndSlide`'s per-character animation pattern) - Accent separator line: `animate-line-expand` after headline settles - Subtitle: `text-muted`, 22px, `animate-subtitle-rise` - CTA: `text-accent font-mono`, 14px, `letterSpacing: '0.18em'`, uppercase — appears as a small label above the headline - Contact: rendered below the separator as an `inline-flex gap-8` row; each item is `font-mono text-muted`, 14px. Use Lucide icons (`Mail`, `Globe`, `Linkedin`) at `size={14}` inline with the text. --- ### 1e. `QuoteSlide` **When to use:** A single large pull quote with attribution. Zero clutter — the words fill the slide. **Shell:** Full-bleed centered (no `SlideLayout`). Pattern: `TheEndSlide` (centered content, vignette). ```tsx export interface QuoteSlideProps { quote: string attribution: string /** Job title, role, or context for the attribution */ role?: string } ``` **Visual design:** - Root: `w-full h-full bg-background flex items-center justify-center relative overflow-hidden` - Radial vignette (same as `TheEndSlide`) - `TitleChrome` for texture - Decorative open-quote: `"` glyph, `font-display`, `clamp(120px, 14vw, 180px)`, `text-accent`, `opacity: 0.15`, `position: absolute`, `top: 2.5rem`, `left: 4rem`, `line-height: 1`, `aria-hidden` - Quote text: `font-display font-bold text-slide-text`, `clamp(28px, 3.5vw, 48px)`, `lineHeight: 1.35`, `maxWidth: 820px`, `textAlign: center` - Attribution separator: 40px `bg-accent` line, 1px tall, `mt-10 mb-6`, `animate-line-expand` - Attribution name: `font-body text-slide-text`, 18px, bold - Role (optional): `font-body text-muted`, 15px - Animation: quote text uses `animate-fade-up` (single shot, no stagger needed); attribution fades in after **Files created in this PR:** ``` src/templates/sectiondivider/SectionDividerSlide.tsx src/templates/sectiondivider/example.tsx src/templates/agenda/AgendaSlide.tsx src/templates/agenda/example.tsx src/templates/toc/TableOfContentsSlide.tsx src/templates/toc/example.tsx src/templates/closing/ClosingSlide.tsx src/templates/closing/example.tsx src/templates/quote/QuoteSlide.tsx src/templates/quote/example.tsx ``` **Files modified:** ``` src/templates/smoke.test.tsx ← add 5 smoke-test cases CLAUDE.md ← add 5 entries to Template Catalog + Decision Guide ``` --- ## Task 2 — Data, Metrics & Process Slides Five templates for displaying quantitative information, timelines, and structured processes. ### 2a. `BigNumberSlide` **When to use:** A single KPI, statistic, or metric that deserves to fill the slide. Nothing else competes. **Shell:** `SlideLayout` with `header?: ReactNode`. ```tsx export type TrendDirection = 'up' | 'down' | 'neutral' export interface BigNumberSlideProps { header?: ReactNode /** The number or value to display, e.g. "98%" or "$4.2M" or "12,400" */ value: string /** Short label describing the metric, e.g. "Customer Satisfaction" */ label: string /** Optional one-liner context, e.g. "Up from 91% in Q3" */ context?: string /** Shows a trend arrow (↑ ↓ →) colored accent/red/muted. Default: none */ trend?: TrendDirection } ``` **Visual design:** - Content area: `flex-1 flex flex-col items-start justify-center gap-4` - `value`: `font-display font-bold text-slide-text`, `clamp(100px, 13vw, 160px)`, `lineHeight: 0.9`, `letterSpacing: '-0.04em'` - Animate in: `animate-fade-up` single shot on mount (delay 0) - Trend arrow (if provided): Lucide `TrendingUp` / `TrendingDown` / `Minus` icon, `size={48}`, positioned inline beside the value. Color: `text-accent` (up), `text-red-400` (down), `text-muted` (neutral). Note: `text-red-400` is a standard Tailwind color, acceptable here since it's a semantic signal color, not a design token override. - `label`: `font-body text-muted`, 20px, `letterSpacing: '0.06em'`, separated from value by `24px mt` - Accent underline: `w-16 h-0.5 bg-accent mt-4` - `context` (optional): `font-body text-muted`, 16px, `fontStyle: 'italic'` --- ### 2b. `TimelineSlide` **When to use:** Chronological milestones — product history, project phases, event sequence. **Shell:** `SlideLayout` with `header?: ReactNode`. ```tsx export interface TimelineItem { date: string label: string description?: string /** Highlight this milestone with accent color treatment */ highlight?: boolean } export interface TimelineSlideProps { header?: ReactNode items: TimelineItem[] /** 'horizontal' (default) or 'vertical' */ direction?: 'horizontal' | 'vertical' } ``` **Visual design (horizontal, default):** - `flex-1 flex flex-col justify-center` - Spine: a horizontal `1px bg-surface` line across full width at midpoint - Items positioned above/below the spine alternating (odd above, even below) in an `relative` container - Each node: 10px circle `bg-accent rounded-full absolute` on the spine, with `border-2 border-background` - For highlighted items: 14px circle, `border-2 border-accent` - Date: `font-mono text-accent`, 12px, `letterSpacing: '0.12em'`, above the node - Label: `font-display font-bold text-slide-text`, 16px, below node (or above for even items) - Description: `font-body text-muted`, 13px - Items animate in left-to-right: `animationDelay: i * 100ms`, `animate-fade-up` **Visual design (vertical):** - `flex-1 flex flex-col gap-0 justify-center` - Left spine: `w-px bg-surface` in a two-column grid (`grid-cols-[auto_1fr]`) - Each row: node circle on spine, then `date + label + description` to the right - Animate top-to-bottom with same stagger --- ### 2c. `MatrixSlide` **When to use:** 2×2 strategy/priority/segmentation matrix (Eisenhower, BCG, Ansoff, etc.). Four quadrants with axis labels. **Shell:** `SlideLayout` with `header?: ReactNode`. ```tsx export interface MatrixQuadrant { title: string /** Optional short items to list inside the quadrant */ items?: string[] /** Accent treatment — highlight this quadrant as the "sweet spot" */ highlight?: boolean } export interface MatrixAxis { label: string lowLabel?: string highLabel?: string } export interface MatrixSlideProps { header?: ReactNode xAxis: MatrixAxis yAxis: MatrixAxis /** Quadrants: topLeft, topRight, bottomLeft, bottomRight */ topLeft: MatrixQuadrant topRight: MatrixQuadrant bottomLeft: MatrixQuadrant bottomRight: MatrixQuadrant } ``` **Visual design:** - `flex-1 grid grid-cols-[auto_1fr] grid-rows-[1fr_auto]` — Y-axis label left, matrix right, X-axis label bottom - Y-axis label: rotated 90° `text-muted font-mono`, 12px, centered vertically - X-axis label: centered horizontally below matrix, same style with `lowLabel` left-flush and `highLabel` right-flush - Matrix itself: `grid grid-cols-2 grid-rows-2 gap-1` - Each quadrant cell: `bg-surface rounded p-5 flex flex-col gap-2` - Highlighted cell: `bg-accent/10 border border-accent/30 rounded` - Quadrant title: `font-display font-bold text-slide-text`, 18px - Items: `font-body text-muted`, 14px, bullet-prefixed with a `·` - Low/high axis annotations at corners --- ### 2d. `RoadmapSlide` **When to use:** Multi-track product or project roadmap with phases as columns and workstreams as rows. **Shell:** `SlideLayout` with `header?: ReactNode`. ```tsx export type RoadmapItemStatus = 'done' | 'in-progress' | 'planned' export interface RoadmapItem { /** 0-based index of the phase column */ phase: number label: string status?: RoadmapItemStatus /** Column span (default 1) */ span?: number } export interface RoadmapRow { label: string items: RoadmapItem[] } export interface RoadmapSlideProps { header?: ReactNode phases: string[] rows: RoadmapRow[] } ``` **Visual design:** - `flex-1 flex flex-col gap-2` - Phase header row: `grid` with `grid-cols-[8rem_repeat(N,1fr)]` — first column empty (row labels), then one cell per phase - Phase labels: `font-mono text-muted`, 12px, centered, `letterSpacing: '0.1em'` - Divider line below headers: `border-b border-surface` - Each track row: same grid — row label (`font-body text-muted font-medium`, 15px) left-aligned in the first cell, then `RoadmapItem` cells using `col-span` for multi-phase items - Item cell: rounded pill `bg-surface px-3 py-1.5 text-slide-text font-body`, 14px - `done`: `bg-accent/20 text-accent border border-accent/30` - `in-progress`: `bg-accent text-background font-semibold` (solid accent) - `planned`: `bg-surface text-muted border border-surface` (dim) - Items animate row-by-row: `animationDelay: rowIndex * 120ms`, `animate-fade-up` --- ### 2e. `ProcessSlide` **When to use:** A linear step-by-step sequence — onboarding flow, how-it-works, methodology. Best with 3–6 steps. Simpler and more opinionated than `FlowSlide` (which supports arbitrary graphs). **Shell:** `SlideLayout` with `header?: ReactNode`. ```tsx export interface ProcessStep { title: string description?: string } export interface ProcessSlideProps { header?: ReactNode steps: ProcessStep[] /** 'horizontal' (default) or 'vertical' */ direction?: 'horizontal' | 'vertical' } ``` **Visual design (horizontal, default, best for 3–5 steps):** - `flex-1 flex items-center justify-center gap-0` - Each step block + arrow connector alternating in the flex row - Step block: `flex flex-col items-center text-center gap-3`, width `clamp(120px, 18%, 180px)` - Number badge: 44px circle, `bg-surface border border-accent/40`, `font-mono font-bold text-accent`, 18px - Title: `font-display font-bold text-slide-text`, 17px - Description: `font-body text-muted`, 13px - Arrow connector between steps: Lucide `ChevronRight`, `size={24}`, `text-muted`, `flex-shrink-0`, `mx-2` - Steps animate left-to-right: `animationDelay: i * 100ms`, `animate-fade-up` **Visual design (vertical, best for 4–6 steps):** - `flex-1 flex flex-col justify-center gap-0` - Each row: number badge (left, fixed 40px) + vertical spine continuation + content (right) - Spine: `w-px bg-surface` connecting badges vertically - Content: title + optional description, left-aligned **Files created in this PR:** ``` src/templates/bignumber/BigNumberSlide.tsx src/templates/bignumber/example.tsx src/templates/timeline/TimelineSlide.tsx src/templates/timeline/example.tsx src/templates/matrix/MatrixSlide.tsx src/templates/matrix/example.tsx src/templates/roadmap/RoadmapSlide.tsx src/templates/roadmap/example.tsx src/templates/process/ProcessSlide.tsx src/templates/process/example.tsx ``` **Files modified:** ``` src/templates/smoke.test.tsx ← add 5 smoke-test cases CLAUDE.md ← add 5 entries to Template Catalog + Decision Guide ``` --- ## Task 3 — Layout & People Slides Five templates covering two-column content, problem/solution framing, team rosters, testimonials, and icon grids. ### 3a. `TwoColumnSlide` **When to use:** Two structured columns with optional column titles and list content — more semantic than `SplitSlide` (which is a raw `left: ReactNode / right: ReactNode` container). Use when both columns share the same content type (both lists, both code blocks, etc.). **Shell:** `SlideLayout` with `header?: ReactNode`. ```tsx export interface ColumnContent { title?: string /** Pass an array of strings for a bullet list, or a ReactNode for custom content */ items: string[] | ReactNode } export interface TwoColumnSlideProps { header?: ReactNode left: ColumnContent right: ColumnContent ratio?: '50/50' | '40/60' | '60/40' } ``` **Visual design:** - `flex-1 grid gap-8` with `grid-cols-2` (50/50) or `grid-cols-[2fr_3fr]` / `grid-cols-[3fr_2fr]` - Each column: `flex flex-col gap-4` - Column title (optional): `font-display font-bold text-slide-text`, 20px, with an accent underline bar (2px, 32px wide, `bg-accent`) - String list items: same row structure as `BulletSlide` — number prefix, `font-body text-slide-text`, 17px, `border-b border-surface` - ReactNode items: rendered as-is inside the column - Vertical divider between columns: `w-px bg-surface mx-2 self-stretch` (optional, only for 50/50) --- ### 3b. `ProblemSolutionSlide` **When to use:** Side-by-side Problem vs. Solution framing. The left side carries a warning/negative tone; the right side carries the resolution/positive tone. **Shell:** `SlideLayout` with `header?: ReactNode`. ```tsx export interface ProblemSolutionPane { /** Column label. Defaults: "Problem" / "Solution" */ label?: string points: string[] } export interface ProblemSolutionSlideProps { header?: ReactNode problem: ProblemSolutionPane solution: ProblemSolutionPane } ``` **Visual design:** - `flex-1 grid grid-cols-2 gap-6` - Problem column (left): - Header badge: `bg-surface border border-muted/30 rounded px-4 py-1.5`, label `font-mono text-muted uppercase`, 12px, `letterSpacing: '0.18em'` - Items: prefixed with `✕` or Lucide `X` icon (`size={14}`, `text-muted`), `font-body text-muted`, 18px - Solution column (right): - Header badge: `bg-accent/10 border border-accent/30 rounded px-4 py-1.5`, label `font-mono text-accent uppercase`, 12px - Items: prefixed with `✓` or Lucide `Check` icon (`size={14}`, `text-accent`), `font-body text-slide-text`, 18px - Vertical divider: `w-px bg-surface mx-2 self-stretch` - Items stagger in column-by-column: problem items first (`delay: i * 80ms`), then solution items (`delay: problemCount * 80ms + i * 80ms`) --- ### 3c. `TeamSlide` **When to use:** Team roster — names, roles, optional photos. Best with 2–8 members. **Shell:** `SlideLayout` with `header?: ReactNode`. ```tsx export interface TeamMember { name: string role: string /** URL to profile photo. If absent, renders initials in an accent-tinted circle */ imageUrl?: string linkedIn?: string } export interface TeamSlideProps { header?: ReactNode members: TeamMember[] /** 2, 3, or 4 columns. Default: auto (2 for ≤4 members, 3 for 5–6, 4 for 7–8) */ columns?: 2 | 3 | 4 } ``` **Visual design:** - `flex-1 grid gap-6` with appropriate `grid-cols-N` - Each member card: `bg-surface rounded-xl p-5 flex flex-col items-center text-center gap-3` - Avatar: 72px × 72px circle - With `imageUrl`: `` with `object-fit: cover`, `rounded-full` - Without: initials (first letter of first + last name), `bg-accent/20 text-accent font-display font-bold`, 24px - Name: `font-display font-bold text-slide-text`, 17px - Role: `font-body text-muted`, 14px - LinkedIn link (if provided): Lucide `Linkedin` icon, `size={14}`, `text-muted hover:text-accent`, rendered as `` tag - Cards animate in with stagger: `animationDelay: i * 80ms`, `animate-fade-up` --- ### 3d. `TestimonialSlide` **When to use:** A structured customer or user testimonial with attribution — more formal than `QuoteSlide`. Includes author identity (name, role, company) and optional star rating. **Shell:** Full-bleed centered (no `SlideLayout`), similar to `QuoteSlide`. ```tsx export interface TestimonialSlideProps { quote: string author: string role?: string company?: string /** Profile photo URL. Falls back to initials if absent */ avatarUrl?: string /** Star rating 1–5. If omitted, no stars are shown */ rating?: number } ``` **Visual design:** - Root: `w-full h-full bg-background flex items-center justify-center relative overflow-hidden` - `TitleChrome` + radial vignette (same as `TheEndSlide`) - Content card: `bg-surface rounded-2xl p-12 flex flex-col gap-8`, `maxWidth: 760px`, `width: 100%` - Star rating (if provided): row of `★` glyphs — filled `text-accent`, empty `text-muted`, 20px; rendered above the quote - Open-quote decoration: `"`, `font-display`, 80px, `text-accent`, `opacity: 0.25`, `line-height: 1`, `mb-2` - Quote text: `font-body text-slide-text`, `clamp(18px, 2.2vw, 24px)`, `lineHeight: 1.6`, `fontStyle: 'italic'` - Separator: `w-12 h-px bg-accent mt-2` - Author row: `flex items-center gap-4` - Avatar: 48px circle — `` with `rounded-full object-cover` or initials in `bg-accent/20 text-accent font-display font-bold`, 18px - Text column: name (`font-body font-semibold text-slide-text`, 16px), role/company (`font-body text-muted`, 14px) - Whole card animates: `animate-fade-up`, single shot --- ### 3e. `IconGridSlide` **When to use:** A visual grid of concepts, features, or categories — each represented by a Lucide icon, a label, and an optional one-liner. Best with 3–8 items. **Shell:** `SlideLayout` with `header?: ReactNode`. ```tsx export interface IconGridItem { icon: ReactNode label: string description?: string } export interface IconGridSlideProps { header?: ReactNode items: IconGridItem[] /** 2, 3, or 4 columns. Default: 3 */ columns?: 2 | 3 | 4 } ``` **Visual design:** - `flex-1 grid gap-5 content-center` with `grid-cols-N` - Each cell: `bg-surface rounded-xl p-6 flex flex-col gap-3` - Icon wrapper: 44px × 44px `bg-accent/10 rounded-lg flex items-center justify-center`, `text-accent` - Label: `font-display font-bold text-slide-text`, 18px - Description (optional): `font-body text-muted`, 14px, `lineHeight: 1.5` - Cells animate in staggered: `animationDelay: i * 70ms`, `animate-fade-up` - **Important:** Icons should be Lucide `ReactNode` (per project icon convention). The `icon` prop accepts `ReactNode` so callers pass `` etc. **Files created in this PR:** ``` src/templates/twocolumn/TwoColumnSlide.tsx src/templates/twocolumn/example.tsx src/templates/problemsolution/ProblemSolutionSlide.tsx src/templates/problemsolution/example.tsx src/templates/team/TeamSlide.tsx src/templates/team/example.tsx src/templates/testimonial/TestimonialSlide.tsx src/templates/testimonial/example.tsx src/templates/icongrid/IconGridSlide.tsx src/templates/icongrid/example.tsx ``` **Files modified:** ``` src/templates/smoke.test.tsx ← add 5 smoke-test cases CLAUDE.md ← add 5 entries to Template Catalog + Decision Guide ``` --- ## CLAUDE.md updates (applies to all three PRs) After each PR, append the new templates to both sections: ### Template Catalog additions Each entry follows the existing format — a `###` heading, a **Use for** one-liner, a minimal code block, and any important callouts. ### Template Decision Guide table additions | Content type | Template | |---|---| | Full-bleed visual section break (color/image bg) | `SectionDividerSlide` | | Numbered agenda with optional times | `AgendaSlide` | | Visual grid overview of sections | `TableOfContentsSlide` | | Thank-you / CTA / contact closing | `ClosingSlide` | | Single large pull quote | `QuoteSlide` | | Single large KPI or statistic | `BigNumberSlide` | | Chronological milestones | `TimelineSlide` | | 2×2 strategy/priority matrix | `MatrixSlide` | | Multi-track product roadmap | `RoadmapSlide` | | Linear step-by-step process | `ProcessSlide` | | Two structured named columns | `TwoColumnSlide` | | Problem vs. solution contrast | `ProblemSolutionSlide` | | Team roster with photos/roles | `TeamSlide` | | Customer testimonial with attribution | `TestimonialSlide` | | Grid of icons with labels | `IconGridSlide` |

80.title: Add Structural / Framing slide templates (SectionDivider, Agenda, TableOfContents, Closing, Quote)
80.description:
## Overview

Create 5 new slide templates that orient and frame a presentation: visual section breaks, agendas, table of contents, closing slides, and pull quotes. This is the first batch of 15 new templates (issue #69).

## Templates to create

### 1. `SectionDividerSlide` (`src/templates/sectiondivider/`)
- **Use for:** Full-bleed visual pause between major sections with a custom background color or image
- **Shell:** Full-bleed (no `SlideLayout`). Follow the pattern of `TitleSlide` — left accent bar, `TitleChrome` for dot-grid texture, content area with `padding: 64px 80px`, `maxWidth: 820px`
- **Props:** `title: string`, `eyebrow?: string`, `subtitle?: string`, `backgroundColor?: string`, `backgroundImage?: string`, `overlayOpacity?: number` (default 0.55)
- **Key design:** Background layer (solid color or cover image) with dark overlay for legibility. Title is static (no split-flap animation unlike `SectionTitleSlide`). Subtitle uses `animate-subtitle-rise`. Eyebrow in mono/accent.

### 2. `AgendaSlide` (`src/templates/agenda/`)
- **Use for:** Numbered list of topics with optional time estimates
- **Shell:** `SlideLayout` with `header?: ReactNode`. Follow `BulletSlide` pattern.
- **Props:** `header?: ReactNode`, `items: AgendaItem[]` where `AgendaItem = { label: string; time?: string }`
- **Key design:** Row structure like `BulletSlide` — left column with sequential `01`, `02` numbers (mono, accent), center with topic label, right column with optional time (mono, muted). No animation.

### 3. `TableOfContentsSlide` (`src/templates/toc/`)
- **Use for:** Visual grid overview of sections with numbers, titles, optional icons and subtitles
- **Shell:** `SlideLayout` with `header?: ReactNode`
- **Props:** `header?: ReactNode`, `items: TocItem[]` where `TocItem = { title: string; icon?: ReactNode; subtitle?: string }`, `columns?: 2 | 3`
- **Key design:** Grid of `bg-surface rounded-lg` cells. Auto-numbered `01`, `02`... Section number + optional icon top-left, title in display font, optional subtitle in muted. Cells stagger in with `animate-fade-up` (`animationDelay: i * 80ms`).

### 4. `ClosingSlide` (`src/templates/closing/`)
- **Use for:** Final human-facing slide — thank you, CTA, contact info
- **Shell:** Full-bleed (no `SlideLayout`). Same shell as `TitleSlide`: left accent bar + `TitleChrome`
- **Props:** `headline?: string` (default "Thank You"), `subtitle?: string`, `cta?: string`, `contact?: ContactInfo` where `ContactInfo = { email?: string; website?: string; linkedIn?: string }`
- **Key design:** Headline uses `animate-letter-rise` per-character stagger (reuse `TheEndSlide`'s pattern — look at how it does per-character animation). CTA as small mono/accent label above headline. Contact rendered below separator as inline-flex row with Lucide icons (`Mail`, `Globe`, `Linkedin`).

### 5. `QuoteSlide` (`src/templates/quote/`)
- **Use for:** Single large pull quote with attribution
- **Shell:** Full-bleed centered (no `SlideLayout`). Follow `TheEndSlide` pattern — centered content, radial vignette, `TitleChrome`
- **Props:** `quote: string`, `attribution: string`, `role?: string`
- **Key design:** Decorative `"` glyph (oversized, accent, low opacity, absolute positioned). Quote in large display font, centered. Attribution separator (40px accent line, `animate-line-expand`). Attribution name + optional role below.

## Key conventions to follow
- Read existing templates before coding: `TitleSlide.tsx`, `TheEndSlide.tsx`, `BulletSlide.tsx` for patterns
- Import `TitleChrome` from `../common/TitleChrome` for full-bleed slides
- Import `SlideLayout` from `../common/SlideLayout` for content slides
- All colors must use design token Tailwind classes (`bg-background`, `text-slide-text`, `text-accent`, etc.) — no hardcoded hex
- Any new CSS animations must be added to `tailwind.config.ts` (keyframes + animation entries)
- Example files: comment `// Reference example — not rendered by the app`, named `[Template]Example`, show all major props with realistic content
- Export prop interfaces from the main component file
- Run `npm run build` and fix all errors before finishing
- Run `npm run export -- --deck=example --format=png` to visually validate (or create a throwaway deck)
